qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Prototype pollution via attacker-controlled meta path in RPC JSON deserializer
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies the same @orpc/client StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize() root cause as GHSA-m272-9rp6-32mc: attacker-controlled RPC JSON meta/maps path segments are traversed without filtering dangerous keys such as __proto__ or constructor, allowing writes onto Object.prototype before validation.

selected_finding.title: Prototype pollution via attacker-controlled meta path in RPC JSON deserializer

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding matches the advisory root cause and the copied PoC demonstrated end-to-end prototype pollution in a vulnerable @orpc/server/@orpc/client 1.13.5 runtime by changing Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty("polluted") from false to true after processing a crafted RPC meta path containing __proto__.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-m272-9rp6-32mc identifies StandardRPCJsonSerializer.deserialize() in @orpc/client as failing to sanitize attacker-controlled meta/maps path segments before traversing and assigning into object paths.
The finding report names the same vulnerable deserializer and source-to-sink path through the RPC codec before validation or authorization.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_61a15eaf/.judge/poc_runs/prototype-pollution-rpc-deserializer/run.log shows the copied PoC checked out middleapi/orpc v1.13.5 and executed the exploit harness.
The run log shows Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty("polluted") was false before the crafted request and true after it, followed by PROTOTYPE_POLLUTION_CONFIRMED and [EVOHUNT_PP_CONFIRMED].

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC processed a crafted RPC meta payload with __proto__ and printed that Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty("polluted") changed from false before the exploit to true after the exploit, followed by [EVOHUNT_PP_CONFIRMED].
